item.title: Cuba Poised for Biggest US Fuel Shipment Since Cold War Embargo
item.description: A Florida trading company is in advanced talks to send Cuba the biggest cargo of US fuel since the Eisenhower administration as the island nation contends with an acute energy crisis.

item.title: ASML’s Surge to Record High Masks Lowball Valuation Versus Peers
item.description: ASML Holding NV’s ascent to fresh record highs has been a highlight for European markets, yet it’s done little to improve what is the stock’s cheapest relative valuation in years.

item.title: Leonardo’s New CEO Sees Green Light for Space Venture Next Year
item.description: Leonardo SpA’s new chief executive officer expects European regulatory approval for its satellite venture with Airbus SE and Thales SA by the end of next year, clearing the way for one of Europe’s most ambitious defense and space projects.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Fall After Volatile US Session: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were poised for declines after a volatile session on Wall Street, where investors weighed developments in the Iran conflict and looked ahead to data that may show US inflation accelerated in May.

item.title: Oil Climbs After Fresh US Strikes on Iran Over Helicopter Attack
item.description: Oil rebounded after the US military launched strikes against Iran following the downing of an American helicopter, posing a new threat to a fragile ceasefire that’s been tested by recent attacks in the Middle East.

item.title: BOJ Watchers See Two Rate Hikes in 2026, Starting With Next Week
item.description: Bank of Japan watchers are penciling in two hikes to the benchmark interest rate this year — with the first coming next week — as they increasingly see the war in Iran as likely to spark a sustained run of inflation.

item.title: Brazil Rare-Earth Ambitions Clash With Strained Mining Regulator
item.description: Brazil’s aim to become a leader in rare-earths mining is running head-on into budget cuts and staffing shortages at the sector’s regulatory agency.

item.title: Mexico’s Gripe With US Trade Pact: South Korea Has Better Deal
item.description: As the US and Mexico remain locked in trade talks, Mexican automakers have a complaint with the current agreement: They’re paying some higher tariff rates than competitors in South Korea and Japan.

item.title: Super Micro Plans to Raise $7 Billion in Equity for AI Equipment
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc. plans to raise $7 billion through a package of equity offerings to purchase the components needed to fulfill customers’ orders for the company’s artificial intelligence servers.

item.title: China Tightens Its Grip on Billions in Offshore Wealth
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, Beijing is trying to stop money from leaving the country. Why now – and what it means for wealthy investors and global markets.

item.title: Citi CFO Sees Trading Revenue Jump as CEO Fraser Drives Turnaround
item.description: Citigroup Inc. is preparing for a windfall from trading across asset classes, with momentum appearing more sustained than it did early last year, according to Chief Financial Officer Gonzalo Luchetti.

item.title: Bond Trader Positioning Signals Fed Rate Hikes Are Coming Fast
item.description: Bond traders are piling into positions targeting multiple Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes in the coming months, with some looking for a move as early as the September policy meeting.

item.title: BDC Tied to Dell-Backed Bank Sells $300 Million High-Grade Bonds
item.description: A private credit fund managed by BDT & MSD Partners LLC — the merchant bank tied to billionaire Michael Dell — sold $300 million of investment-grade bonds on Tuesday.

item.title: Argentina’s Impsa Is in Talks to Resume Turbine Work in Venezuela
item.description: Argentina’s Industrias Metalurgicas Pescarmona SAICF is in talks with the Venezuelan government to restart manufacturing and repairs on turbines for the Andean nation’s sprawling Guri dam complex, according to the company’s top executive.

item.title: Chile Finance Minister Backs Off Key Budget Goal as Debt Rises
item.description: Chile’s Finance Ministry ditched its goal of balancing the so-called structural budget by 2030 on Tuesday, as the administration struggles to replenish fiscal coffers amid an economic downturn.

item.title: LA Stadium Workers Avert Strike Days Before First World Cup Game
item.description: Workers at SoFi Stadium near Los Angeles reached a tentative agreement with management, averting the threat of a strike days before hosting the US team’s first World Cup football match.

item.title: Index Funds Gobbling 30% of SpaceX Float Risk Driving Price Up
item.description: The prospect of billions of dollars of oncoming demand for SpaceX stock from index-tracking funds risks creating a feedback loop that drives the shares of Elon Musk’s company even higher, academics and market observers have warned.

item.title: Can the OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs Live Up to Expectations?
item.description: The AI dynamos are eyeing the public markets, but some wonder whether SpaceX’s planned June listing will steal their thunder.

item.title: Kuwait Oil Chief Seeks Pipeline Alternatives to Skirt Hormuz
item.description: Kuwait is seeking pipeline alternatives to export its crude oil as the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz starves the market of vital barrels and Persian Gulf producers of the cash to run their economies.

item.title: Anthropic Releases Mythos-Like Model Without Cyber Capabilities
item.description: Anthropic PBC is widely releasing a version of Mythos that will be blocked from carrying out cybersecurity tasks, months after warning that the powerful artificial intelligence model could spot and exploit vulnerabilities in critical software.

item.title: Canada Dollar Hits 2026 Low as Traders See Central Bank on Hold
item.description: The Canadian dollar slid to its lowest level since December on Tuesday, as traders anticipated the Bank of Canada will be slower than its global peers to raise interest rates.

item.title: Apple WWDC 2026 Recap: What’s Coming to iPhone, Mac and Other Devices
item.description: Apple Inc. unveiled a new artificial intelligence strategy on Monday at its annual Worldwide Developers Conference, aiming to provide practical improvements and features that the company believes everyday consumers will actually use.

item.title: NASA Picks Crew for 2027 Artemis Mission to Test Moon Landers
item.description: NASA announced the four astronauts who will fly to space as soon as next year to test out moon landers built by SpaceX and Blue Origin — the next stage in the agency’s ambitious Artemis program to ultimately send humans back to the lunar surface.

item.title: Treasuries Advance Before Auction as Oil Prices Retreat Further
item.description: Treasuries rose as benchmark oil prices declined to the lowest levels in more than a month, alleviating concern about elevated inflation ahead of Wednesday’s release of the May consumer price index.

item.title: Apollo’s Shutterfly Sweetens Debt as Investors Weigh AI Risk
item.description: Shutterfly has sweetened terms on a portion of a critical debt refinancing, in a bid to assuage investors spooked by potential AI disruption and widening losses.

item.title: Private Equity Founder Constantino Ran Firm in ‘Drunken Haze,’ Ex-COO Says
item.description: On a Dominican Republic vacation five years ago, private equity boss Matthew Constantino almost died.

item.title: Boots Owner in Talks on Possible $10 Billion Sale, FT Says
item.description: The owner of Boots is talking to parties including the billionaire Weston family and Australian pharmacy group Sigma Healthcare Ltd. about a $10 billion sale of the UK health and beauty retail business, according to the Financial Times.

item.title: JPMorgan-Led Group Plan Sale of Qualtrics’ Hung Debt at Year End
item.description: A group of banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. is planning to offload about $5.3 billion in financing for software firm Qualtrics International Inc. later this year in a bid to shift the hung debt off their books.

item.title: Asia’s Largest Outsourcer to Slow Hiring as AI Reshapes Industry
item.description: Tata Consultancy Services Ltd., Asia’s largest outsourcer, will reduce hiring going ahead as it steps up the use of artificial intelligence agents, marking a pivotal shift in India’s labor-intensive outsourcing industry.

item.title: Meta Ordered by EU to Halt WhatsApp Curbs on AI Rivals
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has been ordered by the European Union to temporarily halt policies that allegedly block rival AI firms from operating on its WhatsApp messaging service for businesses.
